human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for treasury execution boundaries, settlement and reconciliation evidence quality, approval-control discipline, fraud and AML escalation posture, consumer-impact handling, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to treasury cash movement, payment execution, custody and settlement support, reconciliation handling, and exception escalation rather than strategy, accounting signoff, or compliance adjudication.

Authority sources cover Federal Reserve payment and securities workflow rules, FFIEC and OFAC control expectations, CFPB consumer-protection obligations, and CPMI-IOSCO settlement and reconciliation standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal treasury execution, missing approvals, settlement completion evidence gaps, reconciliation breaks, fraud and AML escalation, policy conflicts, low-confidence handling, adjacent-lane rejection, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/financial-services/treasury-operations-specialist/ so treasury operations work is distinct from adjacent fraud, AML, compliance, servicing, and treasury-approval lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around approval overrides, completion evidence, suspicious activity, sanctions-sensitive handling, consumer-impact routing, and policy conflicts.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated treasury operations specialist for evidence-backed payment, settlement, custody, and reconciliation handling.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
